reviews.review_description: This was one of my first games and I still love it.

Update 8-12-19: Ha!! I have gotten past that part and finished the game! It is awesome, and I hope you make more like it! (Puzzle/adventure/not horror) This is a game I will go back and play.  Next time I  will try to find all the toys.  How many are there?  ;)
original review-I am thoroughly enjoying this game! Lots of playtime and an engaging story. The graphics are excellent. I am stuck at 3-4, it might be s glitch. I can get the first 2 gates to open, but I'm stymied to get the third. I believe I have tried every combination of movements, including dropping the cars multiple times. Is this a glitch or what am I doing wrong?

developer_response.body: Hi there! Glad to hear you are enjoying the game. That level can definitely be a tricky one, but it sounds like you're SO close. If you reach out to us at hello@turbo-button.com, we'd be happy to share a walkthrough video of that level to help you along.

reviews.review_description: So much fun to play but it needs more levels.

So that sucked about my timing lol but you cant be mad at even 9.99$ lol games are expensive to make and they arent asking much there for me is about 10 absolute must own system sellers for me thats all subjective ofcourse but this is 1 of them its so beautiful charming and controls are spot if you cannot find 10$ worth of fun then im not sure what u are doing lol

The game itself is simple and yet so entertaining, the VR effects are subtle but draw you into the game in  comfortable ways that other titles attempt to do so with flashy effects. It’s family friendly and a great introduction to the capabilities and immersion of decent VR.

Hands down one of the top5 games in the platform.Everything about it is just amazing the colors, the graphics, the movement, the puzzles, the details I freaking love it.
Give it a try no matter your age(I'm 41),it worths every cent.

This is a brilliant game, managed to get alot of the levels but really got stuck in the car scrap yard maybe 2 - 3 levels in, I gave up and went to something else. It would be great if you could make alot more like this one. Thanks

reviews.review_title: A timeless classic. One for the ages.
reviews.review_description:
I’ve written a few dozen reviews since I’ve bought my Go, and I feel like I’ve been fairly generous with awarding five stars. If a game exceeded my expectations and I highly recommend it, then it would stand to reason that it should receive the best rating. The only issue there is that it somewhat marginalizes the even rarer occurrence: the classic. The game that will stand the test of time, will age well, will define the medium, will even come to be held in such high regard that you cannot imagine never having played it. That is what you will find with Along Together.

I resisted purchasing it for the longest time, because it looked like merely a game for small children, and I felt a bit silly at almost 40 playing it. After a while, the reviews were hard to ignore, and it went on a small sale, so I decided to take the leap of faith. Just as I said about Eclipse: Edge Of Light, another bona fide VR classic, this game lives up to all of the hype and deserves its just rewards, and then some.

This isn’t just a game for children: this is a game for the child in all of us. Is there a more relatable storyline or humanistic archetype than a tale of a boy or girl (depending on your choice) looking for his or her lost dog? There are no antagonists or enemies in your way, just distance, time, and many, many obstacles. This game magically brings to life that sense of wonder, innocence, and purity.

The graphics and art style are a perfect match for the tone. You feel like you are inside of an old-school Nintendo 64 game, truly inside the game interacting with the environment. The music is gorgeously cinematic - playful and nostalgic, with a touch of melancholy. The audio is minimal, with only the slightest of sounds from your environment and your character. There is no dialogue; the gameplay literally drives the narrative. And the game is all the richer for it.

The puzzles are a triumph of inventiveness. They start out cute and fun, but by the end become mind-expanding. You will certainly give your brain a serious workout trying to decode your next moves. The developers, Turbo Button, really outdid themselves plotting all of these puzzles out and creating each level design around them. You may recall they are the studio responsible for the delightful Pixar-esque Floor Plan. I played and reviewed that one some time back, and I found it to be an endearing puzzler that could be enjoyed by the entire family. They have obviously taken that previous experience and challenged themselves to create a self-actualized puzzle extravaganza. If for no other reason, I would recommend this game squarely to puzzle enthusiasts on those merits alone. 

But the game is so much more than that. It is a true 5-Star title in every way. It can be played and enjoyed by anyone, anywhere, and of any age. It can be played and enjoyed now or thirty years in the future, long after the Oculus Go or perhaps even VR has run its course. I cannot wait until my children are a bit older so they can experience the magic too. 

A very long and hyperbolic review, I know. If you’re still with me, I appreciate it. But this game truly deserves it, and deserves to be played as much as any all-time gaming classic.
More! I want more!
Very good game.  Good control.  Graphics perfect for this game.  But especially a great idea.

It is quite likely a problem with my intelligence and not the game but I have played through two pages of levels (with 100% completion) but there are no more levels available to me. The main menu tells me I am only 68% through the game. What am I missing, someone help!

developer_response.body: Hi! You should be able to click the arrows on either side of the map to switch between regions of the game, or swipe left and right while hovering over the map. The 3rd map should become available after completing the 5th level of the 2nd map. If that's not working for you, please reach out to us at hello@turbo-button.com and we'll look into it!

reviews.review_description:
For adults - it is a great way to spend 2 hours.  Superb game play taht will makes you go Humm  once or twice.  

For kids 10 to 12,  it is a nice way to make them solve logic puzzle.

Yeah.  Buy this - it is a great game
Absolutely fantastic. Hats off
Great game for kids! Enjoy it myself as well tbh :)

I have played this game over and over since I’ve purchased it. Each time is just as fun as the first time. Puzzles are not super complex but you definitely have to think. Some levels are a bit harder than others. I found only a couple glitches but they didn’t hinder game play. This game is fun for all ages.
Nice!!!

Very fun game! I found myself wanting to encourage and talk to the main character as I was guiding her through the world. It's a lot of fun and I'd highly recommend it to other new be users like myself.
Amazing game love it. Controls are top notch. Comfortable and fun.

I like this game… it is fun and challenging. example, it took me a few minutes to figure out that I coulnt kill the crows, just had to move the log up, silly me. this game keeps me wanting more adventures and puzzles to figure out how to move on. really fun game that keeps you wanting to see what is next around the corner
This game is so fun!!!! I love the challenge! It’s an awesome obstacle course for your brain!!!!
